subject: Ecology
title: Indigenous Fire Practice
description: A micro-lesson that makes Indigenous Fire Practice usable.

hook.text: Indigenous peoples managed fire for tens of thousands of years before Western suppression began.

buildup.text: Aboriginal Australians used cool burns to clear undergrowth, promote food plants, and drive game.

discovery.text: Native American burning maintained prairies and open woodlands across North America.

twist.text: When colonizers suppressed indigenous fire, fuel built up and megafires became more common.

climax.text: Land managers now partner with indigenous communities to revive traditional burning practices.

punchline.text: The oldest fire science is also the newest.

quiz.question: What happened when indigenous fire practices were suppressed?
options: Fuel built up and megafires increased
